levels.title: The Wrong Button
levels.prompt: Tap the correct button. Try not to make this a whole personality.

rules.scene_prompt: Tap the object, not an answer list.
rules.feedback_on_wrong_tap: Nope. The label was loud, not right.

solution.explanation: The prompt asks for the correct button, and the correct button is the one labeled WRONG.

failure: You trusted a button label. Historic dingleberry behavior.
The button said correct and your brain saluted.
delay: The answer is waiting. Your courage is still buffering.
scorecard: You beat Level 1. The app is legally required to pretend this is progress.

levels.uqiq_moment: The correct answer is the button labeled WRONG.

levels.title: Move the Word
levels.prompt: Move the word WRONG into the only place a smart person would hide it.

solution.explanation: The wrong word belongs in the Truth Box because this pack is allergic to honest labels.

failure: You moved confidence instead of truth. Boldly wrong, like a tiny executive.
Wrong object, wrong box, fully on brand.
delay: Try moving the suspicious word before it unionizes.
scorecard: You moved one word and called it spatial reasoning. Inspiringly small.

levels.uqiq_moment: The player must move WRONG into the Truth Box instead of trusting RIGHT.

levels.title: Type Nothing

rules.placeholder: hint: the answer is not empty
rules.scene_prompt: Fill the answer slot with the literal word the prompt asks for.

solution.explanation: The prompt asks for the word nothing, not a blank answer.

failure: You gave the void a keyboard and still missed.
Blank confidence. Premium nonsense.
delay: The prompt contains the answer. Terrifying development.
scorecard: You typed nothing. Literally. Somehow this counts as literacy.

levels.uqiq_moment: The correct answer is the literal word nothing.

levels.title: One Square Panicked
levels.prompt: Mark the row containing the square that forgot the pattern.

solution.explanation: Every row repeats one letter except the middle cell, which is a number pretending to be helpful.

failure: The pattern was three rows deep and you still fell into the kiddie pool.
You selected a normal square. Brave commitment to boring.
delay: One tile is wearing a fake mustache. Stare less dramatically.
scorecard: You found the impostor square. The alphabet is shaken.

levels.uqiq_moment: The center cell is 8, breaking a grid where every row otherwise repeats a letter.

levels.title: Blink Tax
levels.prompt: Flash the tiles, hide them, then rebuild the sequence.

rules.scene_prompt: Tap tiles into the recall slots in the flashed order.

solution.explanation: Recall the revealed order, not the shuffled choice order.

failure: Short-term memory left the building and took your shoes.
You remembered vibes. The Level requested facts.
delay: Flash, hide, choose. Three verbs. Monumental workload.
scorecard: You retained three tiny words. Neuroscience has been notified.

levels.uqiq_moment: The choice buttons appear in a different order than the flashed sequence.

levels.title: Ramp for One Braincell
levels.prompt: Draw one line so the ball reaches the cup.

solution.explanation: The ramp is the only drawn line that sends the ball toward the cup.

failure: Gravity looked at your line and quietly resigned.
The ball avoided the cup out of respect for itself.
delay: Draw a ramp. Not a monument to hesitation.
scorecard: You solved fake physics with one line. Newton remains mostly unbothered.

levels.uqiq_moment: The ball only reaches the cup when the player draws a rising ramp instead of a flat line or wall.

levels.title: Arrow Trial
levels.prompt: Tap the arrow that refuses the obvious direction.

rules.feedback_on_wrong_tap: You followed the crowd arrow. Tiny parade, wrong street.

solution.explanation: The prompt asks for the arrow refusing the obvious direction, so LEFT is correct.

failure: You picked the popular arrow. Very brave group project energy.
The obvious direction got your vote. Democracy wept softly.
delay: One arrow is refusing the room. Let that tiny rebel cook.
scorecard: You discovered left after meeting right. Cartography is shaken.

levels.uqiq_moment: The correct arrow is LEFT because it refuses the obvious rightward answer.

levels.title: Tiny Loud
levels.prompt: Tap the quiet answer. The loud one is doing unpaid theater.

rules.feedback_on_wrong_tap: Volume is not wisdom. It is just noise wearing shoes.

solution.explanation: The quiet answer is the button labeled tiny answer, not the loud decoy.

failure: You trusted the loud rectangle. Classic hot breath scholarship.
The shouty button waved a flag and your finger joined the parade.
delay: Tiny does not mean hidden. It means stop letting labels yell at you.
scorecard: You found the small answer. The loud one is filing a complaint.

levels.uqiq_moment: The quiet lowercase answer beats the loud important-looking decoy.

levels.title: No Rotate
levels.prompt: Do not rotate the phone. Type the direction it is already facing.

solution.explanation: The app is portrait-first, so the phone is already facing portrait.

failure: You tried to out-rotate a portrait app. Outstanding furniture behavior.
The phone stayed still. Your confidence did a cartwheel.
delay: It is already the answer. The screen is practically pointing at itself.
scorecard: You obeyed the no-rotate sign. This counts as discipline somehow.

levels.uqiq_moment: The answer is the current portrait orientation, not a physical rotation.

levels.title: Finally Sideways
levels.prompt: Fine. Tap SIDEWAYS. Do not turn the phone into a whole event.

rules.feedback_on_wrong_tap: The Level said tap, not enroll in a furniture-moving course.

solution.explanation: The correct move is tapping the button labeled SIDEWAYS, not rotating the device.

failure: You made sideways complicated. Premium dingleberry logistics.
The button was named SIDEWAYS and you still went on a side quest.
delay: The word SIDEWAYS is standing right there, trying not to make eye contact.
scorecard: You ended Pack 1 by tapping a word. The victory parade is very short.

levels.uqiq_moment: The final sideways gag is a tap target, not an orientation change.
